back_translation: Solomon conscripted 70,000 burden-bearers, 80,000 stonecutters in the mountains, and appointed 3,600 chief overseers to supervise them.
Solomon sent a royal letter to Hiram king of Tyre, saying, “Deal with me as you dealt with David my father, to whom you sent cedar wood to build a palace for him to dwell in.

Behold, I am about to build a house for the name of the LORD my God, to consecrate it to Him, to burn fragrant incense before Him, to set out the bread of the Presence regularly, and to offer burnt offerings morning and evening, on the Sabbaths, the new moons, and the appointed feasts of the LORD our God. This is a perpetual ordinance for Israel.
The house that I am about to build is great, for our God is greater than all gods.
But who is able to build Him a house, since the heavens and the highest heavens cannot contain Him? And who am I that I should build Him a house, except to burn sacrifices before Him?

Now send me a craftsman skilled in working gold, silver, bronze, iron, purple, crimson, and blue cloth, and skilled in engraving designs, to work with the skilled men who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, whom David my father provided.
Send me also cedar, cypress, and algum wood from Lebanon, for I know that your servants are skilled in cutting the timber of Lebanon; and behold, my servants will work with your servants,
to prepare timber for me in abundance, for the house I am about to build will be great and wonderful.
And behold, I will give to your servants, the woodcutters who cut the timber, 20,000 cors of ground wheat, 20,000 cors of barley, 20,000 baths of wine, and 20,000 baths of oil.
Then Hiram king of Tyre answered in writing and sent word to Solomon: “Because the LORD loves His people, He has made you king over them.”

And Hiram said further, “Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who made the heavens and the earth, who has given to King David a wise son, endowed with prudence and understanding, who will build a house for the LORD and a royal palace for himself.”
Now I have sent a skilled man, endowed with understanding, namely Huram-abi,

He is the son of a woman from the tribe of Dan, and his father is a man of Tyre. He is skilled in working gold, silver, bronze, iron, stone, wood, purple, blue, fine linen, and crimson, and in every kind of engraving, and in executing any design assigned to him. He will work with your skilled men and the skilled men of my lord David, your father.
Now therefore let my lord send to his servants the wheat, the barley, the oil, and the wine that he has spoken of.
As for us, we will cut timber from Lebanon, as much as you need, and bring it to you as rafts by sea to Joppa; then you may take it up to Jerusalem.
Solomon counted all the foreign men who were in the land of Israel, after the census that David his father had taken, and they were found to be 153,600.
He assigned 70,000 of them to be burden-bearers, 80,000 to be stonecutters in the mountains, and 3,600 to be chief overseers to keep the people working.
